_meta.description: Curated historical macro regimes with realized return numbers. Used by second_order_thesis module to ground LLM-picked analogs in actual data instead of vibes. Each entry is keyed by a short slug. The LLM returns a slug (or free-form analog name that we best-match against this library); the app looks up real numbers.
_meta.source_notes: Returns are approximate peak-to-peak or start-to-peak for the dominant theme of each regime, drawn from published index/sector performance data. Max drawdown is the deepest intra-period trough.

1999_telco_capex.name: 1999 telco capex euphoria
1999_telco_capex.note: Dominant names rallied ~2-3x then collapsed 80%+ in 2000-02. Lesson: capex cycles overshoot then correct violently. Survivors were diversified (VZ) not pure plays.

2019_semis_cycle.name: 2019 semiconductor upcycle
2019_semis_cycle.note: Broad semis (SOXX) doubled. High-quality leaders (NVDA, AMD, AVGO) 3-5x. Clean cycle — no blowup — because end-demand was real.

2003_2007_commodities.name: 2003-07 commodity supercycle
2003_2007_commodities.note: Oil $25 → $140. Copper 3x. Miners 4-5x. Ended in 2008 crash but fundamentals were real. 3rd/4th-order plays (drillers, service cos, shipping) outperformed primaries.

2009_qe1_reflation.name: Post-GFC QE1 reflation
2009_qe1_reflation.note: Everything rallied. High-beta, low-quality most of all. 4th-order plays (small-cap leverage) delivered 5-10x. Lesson: in policy reflation, size the junk tail.

2016_2018_tax_cut.name: 2017 tax cut + synchronized growth
2016_2018_tax_cut.note: Buyback machine. Financials and cyclicals led. Ended in Q4 2018 vol spike. Regime template for rates-up-but-growth-also-up.

2020_covid_stimulus.name: 2020-21 COVID stimulus wave
2020_covid_stimulus.note: SPAC/ARK/crypto 3-10x then 70-90% drawdown. High-quality compounders (MSFT, GOOGL) cleaner 2x. Lesson: stimulus-driven moves are violent both ways; position sizing matters more than picking.

1970s_commodity_stagflation.name: 1970s commodity stagflation
1970s_commodity_stagflation.note: Gold 20x, oil 10x, but SPX flat real. Nominal winners were commodities, real-asset proxies. Equities outside energy/materials were a trap. Template for stagflation regimes.

1980s_productivity_disinflation.name: 1980s disinflation + productivity boom
1980s_productivity_disinflation.note: Long-duration assets dominated. Tech emergence (MSFT, ORCL IPO-era). LBO wave on cyclicals. Template for rates-down-in-expansion.

1995_1999_dotcom.name: 1995-99 internet build-out
1995_1999_dotcom.note: Pure plays 10-50x then -80 to -95%. Picks-and-shovels (CSCO, INTC, SUN) 5-10x, still took 60%+ drawdowns. Quality (MSFT) held better. Template for secular tech booms.

2011_2012_qe2_euro_crisis.name: 2011-12 Europe crisis + QE2
2011_2012_qe2_euro_crisis.note: Choppy. Winners were US-centric high-quality + defensive yield. Avoid European financials, EM. Template for late-cycle fear without crash.

2014_2016_oil_crash.name: 2014-16 oil crash + EM pain
2014_2016_oil_crash.note: Energy -55%, high-yield -20%, EM -30%. Dollar hoarders and bond-proxy quality won. Classic deflation-scare template.

2022_inflation_shock.name: 2022 inflation + rate shock
2022_inflation_shock.note: Long-duration tech -35 to -70%. Energy +60%. Classic value > growth rotation. 2nd-order: drillers, shipping, defense. Template for hiking regimes.

2023_ai_mania_onset.name: 2023 AI capex awakening
2023_ai_mania_onset.note: NVDA +800%, AI-adjacent semis 2-3x, then power/grid 2nd-order emerged. 3rd-order (cooling, utilities) starting to rerate. Closest real-time analog for current regime.

2001_2002_bear.name: 2001-02 post-dotcom bear
2001_2002_bear.note: Tech -78%, SPX -49%. Gold, healthcare, defense held. Template for capex-cycle unwinds. Second-order plays (suppliers) got crushed worst.

2006_2007_housing_peak.name: 2006-07 housing/credit peak
2006_2007_housing_peak.note: Late-cycle meltup, then 2008 crash. Lesson: positive returns in final innings can look like opportunity but mask brittleness. Template for euphoria-gating.
